per_turn_leaks.student_message: In cases like yours, associated symptoms are critical. Are you experiencing any sweating, nausea, or other symptoms during episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yes, I have been getting some night sweats and my partner has mentioned that I've been snoring more loudly than usual at night. But besides those things, I haven't really noticed anything else out of the ordinary during my episodes.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I need you to be completely honest with me. Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, there have been a few instances where I've felt some sharp pains on my right side when I'm coughing or taking deep breaths, but it's not constant and it only happens occasionally. The pain isn't exactly in my chest, more so towards my right shoulder or upper back. It's hard to explain, but it feels like it's radiating from that area.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats? This is important for ruling out infection.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've had a low-grade fever that's been around 38.2°C (100.8°F), but it's only come on intermittently and seems to be worse in the evenings. And yes, I have been experiencing night sweats - it's like my body is just really hot at night, even when the room is cool.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Family history is essential for your assessment. Has anyone in your immediate family had serious medical conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mother has asthma, and my maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer. But that's about it - nothing too out of the ordinary.
